heading: Accreditation or approval required in order to provide adoption services in cases subject to the Convention
label: 14921 Accreditation or approval required in order to provide adoption services in cases subject to the Convention
notes: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this title”, meaning title II of , , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of title II to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 106–279114 Stat. 830Oct. 6, 2000

Effective DateSection effective upon entry into force for the United States of the Convention on Protection of Children and Co-operation in Respect of Intercountry Adoption, pursuant to Article 46(2)(a) of the Convention, with transition rule, see section 505(a)(2), (b) of , set out as an Effective Dates; Transition Rule note under .Pub. L. 106–279section 14901 of this title
amendments: References in TextThis subchapter, referred to in subsec. (a), was in the original “this title”, meaning title II of , , , which is classified principally to this subchapter. For complete classification of title II to the Code, see Tables.Pub. L. 106–279114 Stat. 830Oct. 6, 2000

children.text: is accredited or approved in accordance with this subchapter; or
is providing such services through or under the supervision and responsibility of an accredited agency or approved person.

children.heading: Background studies and home studies
children.label: 1 Background studies and home studies
children.text: The performance of a background study on a child or a home study on a prospective adoptive parent, or any report on any such study by a social work professional or organization who is not providing any other adoption service in the case, if the background or home study is approved by an accredited agency.

children.heading: Child welfare services
children.label: 2 Child welfare services
children.text: The provision of a child welfare service by a person who is not providing any other adoption service in the case.

children.heading: Legal services
children.label: 3 Legal services
children.text: The provision of legal services by a person who is not providing any adoption service in the case.

children.heading: Prospective adoptive parents acting on own behalf
children.label: 4 Prospective adoptive parents acting on own behalf
children.text: The conduct of a prospective adoptive parent on his or her own behalf in the case, to the extent not prohibited by the law of the State in which the prospective adoptive parent resides.
